1.8 In addition with the foregoing PAJEs, it is recommended that
Management instruct the Inventory Committee responsible for the
activities of the one-time cleansing of PPE under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 to exert full effort for the completion thereof for a fair presentation of
the City’s PPE.

2.4 We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to
observe the disclosure requirements by various IPSAS provisions in the
preparation of the financial statements to meet the completeness of
information so that users of the FS could evaluate the significance of the
accounts.

3.9 We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to
coordinate with the City Treasurer for the immediate transfer of the cash
balance amounting to ₱8,583,365.77 to the TF depository bank account
and henceforth for all unexpended balances relating to QRF and MOOE
under the LDRRMF in accordance with Item 5.1.10 of COA Circular No.
2012-002.

4.10 We recommended that Management require the CGSO in coordination
with the CDRRMO to comply with the provisions pertaining to the
receipts and distributions of the donations in-kind received by the City in
order to achieve transparency in the receipt and utilization of the
donations.

5.5 We recommended that Management require the CSWDO in coordination
with the City Treasurer to facilitate the refund of the financial assistance
received by the ineligible beneficiaries and return the amount collected to
the DSWD FO1.

6.10 We recommended that Management instruct the City Engineer to ensure
the completeness and accuracy of the details in DUPA, POW and ABC by
using the prescribed document formats by the DPWH in order to achieve
efficient and well-integrated budgeting for infrastructure projects.

7.9 We recommended that Management demand the return of the excessive
amount from the responsible Engineers who prepared and reviewed the
ABC and the concerned contractors of the two infrastructure projects;
and

7.10 We further recommended that Management instruct the:
a. responsible Engineers to prepare all ABCs in accordance with the
prescribed rules and regulations of DPWH DO No. 197 s. 2016; and
b. City Engineer to conduct a thorough review on the computation of
ABCs to have accurate information to be used in the budgeting of
City’s expenditures.

8.5 We recommended that Management instruct the City Engineer and City
Accountant to maintain a list of completed projects as a reference for
posting the required warranty securities.

8.6 Henceforth, we recommended that Management require the contractors
to post Warranty Securities of completed infrastructure projects with
issued Certificates of Acceptance to guarantee that the they shall perform
their responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 of RA No. 9184.

9.7 We recommended that Management ensure that the BAC, its Secretariat
and TWG members, including other relevant procurement personnel are
sent to attend procurement training or capacity development program
since ultimately the responsibility of the legality of the procurement
project is primarily lodged with the BAC office.

10.8 We recommended that Management instruct the:
a. City Treasurer to provide a copy of the report generated by the
personnel in-charge to the Accounting Office to abate replication
of work; and to transmit the Report of Collections and Deposits to
the Accounting Office weekly; and
b. City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer, to
release the shares on the Barangays RPT collections within five
days after the end of each quarter to enable the barangays to have
additional funds for their operations.

11.13 We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to
require the concerned officials to refund the overpayment of their
COVID-19 hazard pay and ensure that the succeeding payments of
hazard pay and other allowances and benefits adhere to rules and
regulations.

12.4 We recommended that Management instruct the CBSWMB to prepare
the Solid Waste Manual of Procedures to serve as a guide in the efficient
implementation of solid waste management programs and activities.

13.4 We recommended that Management, thru its Local Sanggunian, enact an
ordinance to localize the applicability and ensure the enforcement of the
Safe Spaces Act or RA No. 11313 and its IRR.

14.7 We recommended that Management instruct the City Treasurer and the
CGSO to facilitate the preparation and approval of Contracts of Lease
between the City and all existing stallholders, indicating therein the terms
and conditions agreed upon by the parties in order to support the rental of the
stallholders and to safeguard the interests of both parties.

15.13 We recommended that Management:
a. limit the grant of time extension only to unworkable weather
condition and if any of the valid causes under Section 10.5 of Annex
“E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 is attendant;
b. ensure full coordination with other government agencies, including
the community and private utilities, to achieve effective and more
synchronized planning and implementation of programs and projects;
c. conscientiously carry out the detailed engineering to ensure that issues
such as relocation and viability of the project sites were properly
addressed during the feasibility or preliminary engineering study
prior to project implementation; and
d. in instances of delayed completion, avail of the remedies provided
under Section 8.4 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

16.3 We recommended that Management instruct the:
a) City Budget Officer to propose and coordinate with other offices
concerned for the re-appropriation of the MOOE which were treated
as Continuing Appropriations and cause the reversion of the savings
generated from prior years’ 20% DF completed projects and the re-
appropriation of the accumulated savings to development programs
and projects that need immediate attention; and
b) City Planning and Development Officer to initiate the needed
coordination, monitoring, and evaluation of the implementation of the
PPAs under the 20% DF to assure that all unimplemented PPAs be
given immediate action and implementation or reprogram the funds
of these PPAs to more important developmental projects as required
by current circumstances and trends.

17.8 We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to properly plan
its procurements in accordance with Item 1.2 of Appendix 2 of RA No.
9184 to avoid overstocking of supplies and the eventual spoilage thereof.

We recommended that Management require the:

Inventory Committee to notify and/or inform the Office of the Auditor as to when inventory taking will take place and to undertake actual physical inventory of all properties of the City Government and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI duly reconciled with the Property and Accounting records and submit copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office for verification within the reglementary period prescribed by existing rules and regulations; 


City Accountant to coordinate with the CGSO to prioritize and exert best efforts to reconcile their records as regards properties/inventories to ensure the accuracy, existence, completeness and reliability of the same in the financial statements; and
 
CGSO to employ effective internal control practice in managing the property, plant and equipment of the City by using inventory/property tags.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the CGSO to be guided by the average fuel and oil consumption of the different types of motor vehicles and heavy equipment in authorizing fuel issuance and to require the drivers and operators to properly accomplish the Trip Tickets for every use of the government vehicle or heavy equipment so that the reasonableness of fuel consumption could be properly evaluated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to coordinate with the BAC to:

Comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 on the warranty security for the purchase of goods in the form of retention money or special bank guarantee equivalent to not less than one percent (1%) of the purchase price, to ensure that the supplier will correct any manufacturing defects that may be found within the warranty period; and

Ensure that all disbursements on the procurement of supplies, materials, equipment, and motor vehicles, are adequately documented as set forth in the provisions of Item 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001. 

The Invitations to Bid, Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and Contracts for the procurement of goods, infrastructure projects and services were not posted in the official website of the City Government. In addition, Notices of Award were not posted in the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period. The foregoing practices are not in line with the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, thus, defeating its purpose to establish transparency in the City’s procurement process.

We recommended that Management require the:

BAC Secretariat to post the Invitations to Bid, Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and Contracts in the City Government’s official website and the PhilGEPS website within the prescribed period required in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to ensure transparency in the City’s procurement process; and

City Accountant to ensure that the printout copies of postings in the official website of the City Government are included among the supporting documents for the payment of claim for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects.

Documentary requirements for small value procurement required in Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No.  9184 were not submitted thus, the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions cannot be ascertained.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant to coordinate with the BAC to require the City Government’s suppliers to submit the documentary requirements listed in Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before any payment is made. 

Payment of various goods and infrastructure projects with total cost of ₱87,652,544.10 were not supported with complete documentation contrary to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445, thus, the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions could not be ascertained. 

We recommended that Management instruct the:

BAC to properly evaluate the completeness of Bidding Documents, Eligibility and Technical requirements and other documents before recommending the award of contracts to the City Mayor; and

City Accountant to require all pertinent documents before certifying the disbursement vouchers as to the completeness of supporting documents in line with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 to avoid doubts on the legality, validity and propriety of transactions.

The Minutes on Pre-Bid Conference showed the brand names to be quoted in the proposals of suppliers which is not in adherence to Section 18 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, thus, hindered other prospective bidders/suppliers in submitting their proposals.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC not to indicate the brand names during pre-bid conference in adherence to the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its IRR.

Some deficiencies were noted in the procurement process on the construction of Roadway Slope Protection Sitio 1 at Brgy. Baay, Batac City amounting to ₱489,924.82, in contrary to some of the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184, thus casting doubt as to the validity and propriety of the transaction. 

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC-TWG to strictly scrutinize the technical information/documents submitted by the bidder/s and abide by the procurement timelines laid down in the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for an efficient implementation of programs and projects.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management
take the following courses of action:

a. Undertake actual physical inventory of all properties of the City
Government and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI duly
reconciled with the Property and Accounting records and submit copies
of the report to the Auditor’s Office for verification within the
reglementary period prescribed by existing rules and regulations as
herein discussed;
b. Require the City GSO and the City Accountant to prioritize and exert
best efforts to reconcile their records as regards properties/inventories to
ensure the accuracy, existence, completeness and reliability of the same in
the financial statements;
c. Notify and/or inform the Office of the Auditor as to when inventory
taking will take place in order for us to witness the said activity; and
d. Employ effective internal control practice in managing the property,
plant and equipment of the City by using inventory/property tags.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management
take the following courses of action:

a. Require the City Accountant, City GSO and City Engineer to comply to
the accounting and reporting guidelines as prescribed in COA Circular
2015-008;
b. The Inventory Committee shall conduct an inventory of the local roads
under the jurisdiction of the City and render a Report on the Physical
Count of the Road Network System; and
c. Adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 dated November 25,
2015.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the City
Treasurer submit certified list of taxpayers’ names and the amount due and
collectible for each year as the basis for the City Accountant to make a
Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record an accurate RPT Receivable and
SET Receivable in accordance to Section 20 of MNGAS which is necessary in
order to fairly present the actual financial position of the City Government.

We also recommended that the City Accountant undertake a review of the
RPT and SET Receivable as well as Deferred RPT/SET Income accounts and
take the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect their correct balances.

We recommended that Management require the City Assessor to
prepare an updated Schedule of Fair Market Values and submit the same to
the Sangguniang Panglungsod for the deliberation and passage of the
appropriate ordinance to update the general revision of real property
assessments to authorize the City Government to collect more income from
real property taxes.

a. require official/employees concerned to submit an explanation why the
Construction of the City Inn (Phase 10), Finishing Works (Phase 1) and
Completion of the City Inn (Phase 13) were reported as 100% completed
and Completion of the City Inn (Phase 14) as 85% completed even if they
are not;

b. cause the submission of the following documents for inspection purposes:
 Duly signed Program of Works with Detailed Estimates for the
Construction of the City Inn (Phase 10), Finishing Works (Phase 1)
and Completion of the City Inn (Phase 13).
 Bill of Quantities with Detailed Estimates for the Construction of the
City Inn (Phase 10), Finishing Works (Phase 1) and Completion of the
City Inn (Phase 13).
 As built-plans for Phases with Back-up Computation of as built
quantities, when applicable
 Duly approved complete set of color coded plan showing limit of work
per phase
 Pictures during stages of implementation (before, during, after)

c. Submit justification why management proceeded in the procurement of
construction materials considering that they are aware of the scarcity of
laborers.

a. reorganize the Inspection Committee because the credibility and honesty
of the present members may be found wanting;
b. compute and demand payment of liquidated damages from Caizher Jhon
General Merchandise for the delay in the delivery of the construction
materials; and
c. require officials/employees concerned to submit a written explanation for
failure to observe the established procedure in inspecting and accepting
the delivery of the construction materials.

We recommended that Management indicate the complete
specifications of items to be procured in the Request for Quotation/Bid Form
– Technical Specifications as provided in Sections 17.2 and 18 of R.A. 9184 to
avoid ambiguity in order to obtain the most advantageous price.

We recommended that the Management take the following courses
of action:

a. Inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the
infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity
that the appropriate signboards and/or public notices are already
posted.
b. Programs/Projects/Activities to be implemented by Management are
provided with signboard adopting all the data and specifications
required in COA Circular No. 2013-004 to inform the public of the
essential features/elements of the project in order to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability, encourage
public participation therein and secure the right of people to
information on matters of public concern.

We recommended that Management require the officials concerned
to:

a. submit to the Audit Team the approved Suspension and Resume Orders
of the projects listed in Annex “P” of this report; and
b. comply with the above provisions of laws, rules and regulations on the
submission of approved suspension orders granted together with their
supporting documents within five (5) working days from approval
thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to take the following
courses of action:

a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) should properly evaluate the
completeness of Bidding Documents, Eligibility and Technical
requirements and other documents before recommending the award of
contracts to the City Mayor; and
b. The City Accountant must require all pertinent documents before
certifying the disbursement vouchers as to the completeness of supporting
documents in line with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to avoid doubts on the
legality, validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the employees
responsible for accepting deliveries to notify the Office of the Commission on
Audit of the time and date of scheduled deliveries within 24 hours from such
acceptance to enable us to conduct inspection.

We recommended that Management require the General Service
Office (GSO) and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to furnish the
Auditor’s copy of perfected Contracts and Purchase Orders within five
working days upon perfection thereof for technical review and evaluation in
compliance to COA Circular 2009-001.

We recommended that Management stop paying bonuses and other
compensation of officials and employees through cash advances and
instead directly deposit the amounts to the employees’ respective
bank accounts.

We recommended that Management comply with the prohibition on the
grant of another cash advances when the previous one has not been
settled to avoid exposure of government funds to risk of possible loss or
misappropriation.

We recommended that Management insure all insurable properties of
the city government with the General Insurance Fund administered by the
GSIS to adhere with the provisions of RA 656 and COA Circular No. 92-390
to be able to indemnify in case of damages caused by fortuitous events.

Likewise, comply with the requirements of COA Circular 2018-002 on the
submission of Property Inventory Form (PIF) in order to assist the
implementation of the provisions of RA 656.

We recommended that proper accounting and recording of amount
withheld and remitted be observed to ensure the accuracy and reliability of
the balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth accounts in the
financial statements. Likewise, cause the Accounting Office to determine the
nature of the discrepancies noted and prepare the necessary adjusting
journal entries.

We recommended that Management submit a copy of the history of
repairs of each equipment and motor vehicles subject for repairs as required
under Section 9.1.3.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in order to establish the
propriety and validity of the transactions.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. The City Treasurer and the City Accountant should coordinate with each other and reconcile the discrepancies on their records. Adjust and/or correct the record that needs adjustment for the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
b. Reconcile regularly or at least quarterly the cashbooks with that of the corresponding subsidiary ledger in accordance with the provisions of Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. Undertake actual physical inventory of all properties of the City
Government and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI duly
reconciled with the Property and Accounting records and submit
copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office for verification within the
reglementary period prescribed by existing rules and regulations as
herein discussed;
b. Require the City GSO and the City Accountant to prioritize and exert
best efforts to reconcile their records as regards properties/inventories
to ensure the accuracy, existence, completeness and reliability of the
same in the financial statements;
c. Notify and/or inform the Office of the Auditor as to when inventory
taking will take place in order for us to witness the said activity; and
d. Employ effective internal control practice in managing the property,
plant and equipment of the City by using inventory/property tags.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. Require the City Accountant, City GSO and City Engineer to comply
to the accounting and reporting guidelines as prescribed in COA
Circular 2015-008;
b. The Inventory Committee shall conduct an inventory of the local
roads under the jurisdiction of the City and render a Report on
the Physical Count of the Road Network System; and
c. Adhere to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-008 dated
November 25, 2015.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. The City Treasurer and the City Accountant should coordinate with
each other and reconcile the discrepancies on their records. Adjust
and/or correct the record that needs adjustment for the fair
presentation of the financial statements; and
b. Reconcile regularly or at least quarterly the cashbooks with that of
the corresponding subsidiary ledger in accordance with the provisions
of Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 6.3 of COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

a) Set-up and/or record accounts payable with valid and complete
supporting documents in adherence to Section 157 of the Government
Accounting and Auditing Manual, Volume I, Section 4(6) of
Presidential Decree No. 1445 and PPSAS 1;
b) Present supporting documents of recorded year-end payables to
establish their validity, propriety and existence;
c) Revert prior years’ balances of accounts payable which have
remained in the books of accounts for two years or more in line
with the provision of Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445; and
d) Revisit the existing process of procurement of the City
Government starting from planning, budgeting and accounting and
design an effective monitoring system with the objective of
reflecting the true amount of Accounts Payable duly supported by
proper and necessary documents.

a. Upon collection of the taxes due from the taxpayers, the Management
shall only deduct the basic Real Property Tax and Special Education
Fund Tax collected from the RPT and SEFT Receivable account and
Deferred RPT and SEFT Income;
b. The City Accountant analyze and make necessary entries to reconcile
the discrepancy noted in the SEFT; and
c. The City Accountant make necessary prior period adjustments, to
reflect the correct balances of the receivable and liability account.

We recommended that the City Accountant correctly record the loans
receivable showing the current and non-current portion in order to fairly
present the financial statements of the City Government.

We recommended that:
a. The City Accountant use the correct account in recording government
transactions to avoid misperception as to the nature of the
transactions when viewed by the end users of the financial statements;
b. Management ensure that the equipment be recorded in the books of
the barangays; and
c. Management submit copies of the Deed of Donation.

We recommended that:
a. All disbursements shall be supported by an appropriation made by
law in adherence to Section 305 (a) of R.A. 7160.
b. Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1,
2015 which provides for the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local
Government Units as prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector
Accounting Standards.

We recommended that the City Government:
a. Expedite the transfer of the acquired properties in the name of the
City as required under Section 39(2) of Presidential Decree No.
1445;
b. Refrain from paying transactions without complete documentation
to avoid suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended to Management the following:
a. Require the personnel in charge of the handling and maintenance of
drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies in
the City Health Office to maintain inventory stock cards and ensure
that all issuances are duly accounted and properly documented;
b. Require the City Accountant to submit the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued with proof of actual usage and distribution of drugs
and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies in the City
Health Office as basis in recording the expense account of these two
inventory accounts.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the
Technical Working Group must perform their responsibilities accordingly and
be guided with the DPRI when procuring medicines to ensure economical
procurement as provided under DPRI so that the government can achieve the
best value for money in sourcing essential medicines.

We recommended that concerned employees refund the overpayment.
We further recommended that Management adhere strictly with the provisions
of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2012 on the proper computation of
Subsistence Allowance to prevent overpayment.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
a. See to it that drugs and medicines being procured by the City are
obtained from authorized suppliers, distributors and/or
manufacturers as stated in their License to Operate and List of
Sources;
b. Ensure the authenticity, safety and effectiveness and quality of
procured drugs and medicines by requiring the supplier to submit
together with their bids and/or quotations Certificate of Product
Registration, Certificate of Good Manufacturing Practice and Batch
Release Certificates from Bureau of Foods and Drugs Administration;
c. Adherence to the provisions of Administrative Order No. 43 series
1999, Administrative Order No. 47-A series of 2001 dated August 30,
2001 and Administrative Order No. 103 series 2002 issued by the
Department of Health, Republic Act No. 3720 otherwise known as the
Foods, Drugs and Devices and Cosmetics Act as amended by
Executive Order No. 175, COA Circular 2012-001 and Presidential
Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
a. Submit
- Certificate of Completion of project deliverables;
- Certificate of Acceptance by the agency head of the
deliverables per project component; and
- Duly signed Memorandum of Agreement between the City
Government of Batac and Bureau of Agricultural Research.
b. Strictly adhere to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and
Budget Circular 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007.

We recommended that Management adhere to the principles, rules,
policies, and procedures in granting awards and incentive provided under CSC
MC No. 01 s. 2001, and stop granting death and medical assistance under the
PRAISE program to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management comply with the prohibition on the
grant of another cash advances when the previous one has not been settled.
Further, we recommended that the Accountable Officer be required to liquidate
their respective cash advances within the prescribed period to avoid exposure of
government funds to risk of possible loss or misappropriation.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to Treasury Circular
02-2009 dated August 6, 2009 and effect the increase of fidelity bonds of
accountable officers to amounts corresponding to their maximum cash
accountabilities in accordance with the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates of
Fidelity Bond issued by the Bureau of Treasury, in order to provide adequate
protection on the resources of the City Government.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the mandated use
of government plates and markings and have all motor vehicles owned by the
City Government be marked with “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” and the name
of the City Government written below it to properly identify the vehicles as government
property in adherence to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6 dated
November 7, 1975.

We recommended that the GSO be guided by the average fuel and oil
consumption of the different types of motor vehicles and heavy equipment in
authorizing fuel issuance. Further, require the drivers and operators to properly
accomplish the Trip Tickets for every use of the government vehicle or heavy
equipment so that the reasonableness of fuel consumption could be properly
evaluated.

We recommended that Management support all claim vouchers for
infrastructure projects with pictures of the project, “before”, “during” and
“after” implementation, as required by RA No. 6954.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 on the warranty security for

the purchase of goods in the form of retention money or special
bank guarantee equivalent to not less than one percent (1%) of
the purchase price, to ensure that the supplier will correct any
manufacturing defects that may be found within the warranty
period;
b) Ensure that all disbursements on the procurement of supplies,
materials, equipment, and motor vehicles, are adequately
documented as set forth in the provisions of Item 9.1.3.1 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001.
We recommended and the BAC agreed to require the City
Government’s suppliers to submit the documentary requirements listed
in Annex “H” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 before any payment is
made to avoid audit suspensions.
Again, we recommended the following:
a) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly evaluate
the completeness of Bidding Documents, Eligibility and
Technical requirements and other documents before
recommending the award of contracts to the City Mayor;
b) The City Government shall enter into contract with the
winning bidder, provided, that all documentary requirements
are complied with; and
c) The City Accountant must require all pertinent documents
before certifying the disbursement vouchers as to the
completeness of supporting documents in line with Section 4(6)
of P.D. 1445 to avoid doubts on the legality, validity and
propriety of transactions.
We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from
indicating brand names during pre-bid conference in adherence
provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 and its IRR.
We recommended that the LGU, through its Bids and Awards
Committee, strictly scrutinize the technical information/documents
submitted by the bidder/s and abide by the procurement timelines laid
down in the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 for an efficient implementation of
programs and projects.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
a. Encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the
solid waste management program of the City by educating their
respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or
recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
b. Require the City General Services Officer to comply with the
requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins
with proper labels and proper scheduling of collecting and hauling of
specific types of wastes; and
c. Instruct the City Engineer and City Planning and Development
Officer to provide technical assistance for the establishment and/or
construction of standard-based Materials Recovery Facilities in the
remaining 40 barangays.

We recommended that the CDRRM and the City Accountant prepare
and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis
in accordance with the procedures and requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5
of said COA Circular.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Conduct physical inventory of all properties of the City Government and prepare the corresponding RPCPPE and RPCI duly reconciled with the Property and Accounting records and submit copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office for verification within the reglementary period prescribed by existing rules and regulations as herein discussed; and
Require the City GSO and the City Accountant to reconcile their records as regards properties/inventories to ensure the accuracy, existence, completeness and reliability of the same in the financial statements.

We recommended that the city government:
Exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title in the name of the City as required under Sec. 39 (2) of PD No. 1445; and
Require the City General Services Officer to request from competent authority to appraise the properties without assigned costs so that these could be recorded properly in the books of accounts of the city to fairly present the Land account in the financial statements.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Require the City Treasurer and the City Accountant to coordinate with each other and reconcile the discrepancies in their records. Adjust and/or correct the record that needs adjustment for the fair presentation of the financial statements; and
Reconcile regularly or at least quarterly the cashbooks with that of the corresponding subsidiary ledger in accordance with the provisions of Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

With the foregoing observation, we recommended that management strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 which provides for the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs as prescribed by the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and prepare the necessary adjustments where appropriate, in order to present fairly the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the City Accountant to revert the unutilized LDRRMF for 2012 amounting to P8,824,766.51 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund and shall be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the City Council.

We recommended management, through the City Accountant, to:
Strictly and consistently observe the above-cited provisions of law, rules, and regulations;
File administrative charges  against the concerned officers and/ or personnel, if circumstances warrant; and
Submit on time all the required reports and disbursement vouchers, payrolls together with complete supporting documents to the COA Auditor in order not to delay audit of transactions and preparation of other reports.

We recommended the City Treasurer to:
Maximize her time and effort in the collection of rentals due from market stallholders; and
Implement strictly the provision of Section 4D.01 of the Market and Slaughter Code of the City as regards delinquent market stallholders.

We recommended and management agreed to discontinue from engaging Batac Municipal Employees Multi-Purpose Cooperative in any procurement opportunity so as to avoid conflict of interest in adherence to Section 108 of Presidential Decree No. 1445, Section 1, Rule IX of the Implementing Rules of Republic Act No. 6713, Section 89 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Section 47.1 of the 2016 Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184. Likewise, conduct investigation and if circumstances warrant, file appropriate charges.

We recommended the Bids and Awards Committee to consider and determine properly the essential conditions for Negotiated Procurement – Emergency Cases before recommending the same for approval by the City Mayor in line with Section 53.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 and pertinent provisions of National Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council, Department of Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 for a more transparent and effective implementation of programs of the city government.

We recommended management to ensure strict observance of the provisions of Section 54.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and COA Circular No. 76-41 dated July 30, 1976, with a view of attaining efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations.

We recommended and management agreed to require contractors the submission of materials test(s) results before the conduct of inspection by agency personnel and payment of claims for infrastructure projects so that management will be assured of the quality of materials used.

We recommended the City Government and the City DepEd personnel concerned to refrain from charging expenses that are not within the bounds of laws and regulations on SEF so that the same would be utilized exclusively for the purpose/s it was created for the betterment of public school education.

We recommended and management promised to comply on the following:
Prepare annually the LDRRMFIP in the format provided in Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. The LDRRMFIP shall include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; and
Instruct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer and the City Accountant to prepare and submit a report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis in accordance with the procedures and requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5 of the said COA Circular.

We recommended the City Mayor to:
Encourage active participation of barangay and school officials to support the solid waste management program of the city government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
Require the City General Services Officer to comply with the requirements of the law on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels and proper scheduling of collecting and hauling of specific types of wastes; and
Instruct the City Engineer and City Planning and Development Officer to provide technical assistance for the establishment and/or construction of standard-based Materials Recovery Facilities in the remaining 40 barangays.

We recommended management the following:
Instruct concerned personnel to allocate one percent (1%) of the total Internal Revenue Allotment for the year for LCPC related activities; and
Maximize the utilization of the LCPC funds through implementation of LCPC related programs and projects that will address juvenile issues.
We recommended management to distribute the remaining farm equipments to their intended beneficiaries in order for them to maximize the benefits that could be derived from the usage thereof.

We recommended and management agreed to optimally utilize their 20% Development Fund allocated for the implementation of various programs, projects and activities that are envisioned to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the city and its constituents.
We recommended and management agreed to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through implementation of GAD related programs and projects that will address gender issues and concerns.

observations.recommendation:

a. Create an Inventory Committee to undertake actual physical inventory of
all properties of the City Government and prepare the corresponding
RPCPPE and RPCI duly reconciled with the Property and Accounting
records and submit copies of the report to the Auditor’s Office as well as
to the Mayor’s office within reglementary period prescribed by existing
rules and regulations;
b. Require the City GSO and the City Accountant to prioritize and exert
extra efforts to reconcile their records as regards properties to ensure the
accuracy, completeness, condition and existence of the City Government’s
PPE and Inventories as reflected in the financial statements; and
c. Notify and/or inform the Office of the Auditor on the conduct of inventory
taking by the Inventory Committee to witness the said undertaking.

We have recommended management to perform the following:
a. Exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the
lots through a TCT in the name of the City;
b. Conduct ocular inspection/ physical inventory of all lands owned by the
City Government;
c. Maintain an inventory of all titles confirmed to be in the name of the City
Government and keep them in a safe place; and
d. Require the CGSO to request from competent authority to appraise the
lands acquired through donations or those without assigned costs so that
these could be recorded properly in the books of accounts to fairly present
the Land account in the financial statements.

We have recommended management the following:
a. The City Treasurer and the City Accountant coordinate with each other
and reconcile the discrepancies on their records. Adjust and/or correct the
record that needs adjustment for the fair presentation of the financial
statements; and
b. Reconcile regularly or at least quarterly the cashbooks with that of the
corresponding subsidiary ledger in accordance with the provision of
Section 181 of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual
(GAAM).

We have recommended management the following:
a. The amount of outstanding checks that became stale be immediately
cancelled in pursuance of Section 59 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs
and adjust the affected corresponding accounts; and
b. The recurring and long outstanding reconciling items reflected in the
Bank Reconciliation Statements should be adjusted for an accurate Cash
in Bank account balance of each funds reflected in the financial statements
of the City Government.

We have recommended management to ensure that:
a. Petty Cash Fund should only be used for cash payment of operating
expenses in small amounts which could not be conveniently paid in
checks or which require immediate payment;
b. The provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of
Republic Act 9184 are strictly complied with, particularly the use of
alternative modes of procurement;
c. The provisions of Administrative Order No. 17, series of 2011 in
regard to procurement of common use supplies from the Procurement
Service are adhered to;
d. The number of petty cash funds/petty cash custodians should be
reduced and granted only to duly designated and properly bonded
disbursing officer in the Treasury Office; and
e. Establishment, handling and utilization of the fund should comply
with the requirements of COA Circular No. 97-002 and the Manual
on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs.

We have recommended management to ensure strict observance of the
provisions of Section 54.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of
Republic Act 9184 in the procurement of goods and COA Circular 76-41, with a
view of attaining efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its operations.

We have recommended management the following:
a. Procure common-use supplies at the PS-DBM pursuant to Sections 1
and 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 to be
guaranteed that the cost of procured items are reasonable and
advantageous to management;
b. Other modes of procurement may be resorted to pursuant to the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 if the
required stocks are not available at the PS.

We have recommended management to adhere to the aforementioned
provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act
No. 9184.

We have recommended management the following:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly evaluate the
completeness of Bidding Documents, Eligibility and Technical
requirements and other documents (Section 37.2.3, RA 9184) before
recommending the award of contracts to the City Mayor;
b. The City Government shall enter into contract with the winning
bidder, provided, that all documentary requirements are complied
with; and
c. The City Accountant must require all pertinent documents before
certifying the disbursement vouchers as to the completeness of
supporting documents in line with Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 to avoid
doubts on the legality, validity and propriety of transactions.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Budget Officer control and monitor the preparation and issuance
of Obligation Request (ObR) for every transaction;
b. Obligation Requests be made available prior to the issuance of
Invitation to Bid and should be forwarded to the Head of the Procuring
Entity before entering into a contract to prove that there is available
appropriation and there is authority issued by the Local Chief
Executive (LCE) to incur obligations;
c. Only one Obligation Request (ObR) be prepared and issued for each
procurement contract.

We have recommended and management agreed on the following:
a. The amount of unutilized/unspent PDAF be immediately returned to
the National Treasury in compliance with the Supreme Court ruling;
and
b. Strict adherence to the provisions of Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA
Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and return/refund unused
balances of funds to the Source Agency concerned.

We have recommended management to insure all its insurable properties
with the Government Service and Insurance System pursuant to the provisions
of Section 5 of RA 656 and Section 489 of the Government Accounting and
Auditing Manual (GAAM), Volume I in order for the City Government to be
indemnified in case of possible losses due to manmade or catastrophic events.

We have recommended and management agreed to implement the
following:
a. Stop the practice of investing the Special Education Fund in High Yield
Savings Account in line with the provisions of Section 21 and 22 of COA
Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992; and
b. Utilize the Special Education Fund in accordance with the
programs/priorities cited in Sections 100 (c) and 272 of Republic Act 7160,
otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We have recommended the City Mayor to restrain DepEd officials
concerned from charging expenses that are not within the bounds of law and
regulations on SEF so that the same would be utilized exclusively for the
purpose/s it was created for the betterment of public school education.

We have recommended management to implement projects as planned
and address the causes of the delay of implementation so that the utilization of
such fund as intended would be maximized in order to ultimately achieve
desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the city in
line with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13,
2011.

We have recommended and management promised to comply with the
following:
a. Prepare annually the LDRRMFIP in the format provided in Annex A of

b. List of Programs/Projects/Activities

e. Expected Output
f. Funding Source
g. Amount of Appropriation
The LDRRMFIP shall include under a separate caption, the list of projects

City Accountant to prepare and submit a report on Sources and Utilization
of DRRMF on a monthly basis in accordance with the procedures and
requirements outlined in paragraph 5.1.5 of said COA Circular.

We have recommended the following:
a. The City Government continue its drive against solid waste disposal by
promoting the waste minimization programs and information dissemination
on the proper waste segregation and proper disposal of the same in attaining
cleanliness and keeping a pollution-free environment.
b. The City Government through the City Planning and Development Officer
finalize their 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to
the NSWMCS-DENR for approval. Effective monitoring on the
implementation of the programs, projects and activities of the said SWM
Plan should be undertaken and observe the guidelines provided in the
Republic Act No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program.

a. Require the City Planning Development Officer to allocate not less than
5% of the total appropriations for the year for GAD related activities.
b. Full implementation of GAD Fund in order to realize the intended result
and extending the expected benefits due to the beneficiaries.
c. GAD Focal Person in coordination with the City Budget Officer to

which are consistent, appropriate and responsive to the citys gender
